calendar.matters:
Specially Appearing Respondent Borgwarner Morse Tec Llc As Successor-By-Merger To Borg-Warner Corporations Notice Of Motion To Quash Petition Of Carol Sebastian To Perpetuate Testimony Of Richard Lee Sebastian
Off Calendar Mar-20-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jordan Scott of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and opposing party in Re:Carol Sebastian
John Rosenthal of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant The W.W. Henry Company, L.P.


RULING:
No other appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Specially appearing BorgWarner Morse Tec LLC's motion to quash petition of Carol Sebastian to perpetuate testimony of Richard Lee Sebastian is denied as untimely. BorgWarner failed to provide sufficient notice as required by C.C.P. sections 1005 and 1010.6 and petitioner objected on this ground. The Court did, however, consider BorgWarner's motion as arguments in opposition to the petition. See ruling on the petition. Order signed in open court. Judge: Lynn O'Malley-Taylor, Matter not reported. (503/LOT)

Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition Of Carol Sebastian To Perpetuate Testimony Of Richard Lee Sebastian By Videotaped Deposition
Off Calendar Mar-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jordan Scott of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and moving party in Re: Carol Sebastian

RULING:
No other appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Carol Sebastian's petition to perpetuate testimony of Richard Lee Sebastian by videotaped deposition is granted. The petition is granted as unopposed as to Asbestos Corporation Limited, Union Carbide Corp., Owens-Illinois, Inc., The Pep Boys Manny Moe & Jack of California, Foster Wheeler LLC, Metropolitan Life Insurance Co., Pacific Gas and Electric Co., The W.W. Henry Company, L.P., Cummins Inc. and Stihl Inc. The petition is granted as to BorgWarner Morse Tec LLC because the petition meets the requirements of C.C.P. section 2035.030(b) and does not violate section 2035.010(b). The petition demonstrates that the discovery requested may prevent a failure or delay of justice. (C.C.P. 2035.050(a).) BorgWarner's arguments regarding personal jurisdiction are irrelevant to this petition to perpetuate testimony. (See C.C.P. 418.10; Orr v. City of Stockton (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 622.) BorgWarner's arguments regarding a "second deposition" are misplaced. The deposition may take place at or near Mr. Sebastian's home in Pahrump, NV. Order signed in open court. Judge: Lynn O'Malley-Taylor, Matter not reported. (503/LOT)
